relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Again the crowd has hunger and no food.
Compassion sees three days of staying with him.
Jesus refuses to send them home fainting.
The disciples see impossibility in the deserted place.
Seven loaves are named.
Thanks, breaking, and giving begin the feast.
The little fish are blessed too.
All eat, all are filled, and seven baskets remain.
Four thousand are fed and released.
Immediately they cross by boat.
The Pharisees seek a sign while testing him.
Jesus sighs deeply at a generation seeking proof without trust.
He leaves them and crosses again.
The disciples have one loaf in the boat.
He warns against the leaven of Pharisees and Herod.
They mistake the warning for bread shortage.
Jesus asks why their hearts still do not understand.
Eyes, ears, and memory are all being questioned.
The twelve baskets from five loaves should teach them.
The seven baskets from seven loaves should teach them too.
Still he asks, Do you not understand?
At Bethsaida, a blind man is brought for touch.
Jesus leads him outside and begins a layered healing.
First sight is partial: people like walking trees.
Then clear seeing arrives.
He is sent home away from the village.
On the way, Jesus asks who people say he is.
The answers place him among John, Elijah, and prophets.
Peter names him Messiah.
The confession is held in silence for now.
Jesus teaches that the Son of Humanity must suffer, be rejected, killed, and rise.
Plain speech meets Peter’s resistance.
Jesus names the adversarial function in human thinking that rejects the path.
Following means denying self-rule and taking up the cross.
Life clutched is lost; life surrendered for him and the good message is saved.
The whole world cannot equal the life/soul.
Nothing can be traded for the life/soul.
The Son of Humanity comes in the Father’s glory with holy messengers.
